Tests a specific regression issue related to regular expressions and URI encoding in JavaScript.

# Purpose
This code is a short script that tests the execution of several JavaScript functions and objects within a `try-catch` block. It attempts to create a regular expression using `RegExp("")`, evaluates a URI-encoded string with `eval(encodeURI(isNaN(__proto__)))`, and catches any exceptions that occur during these operations. The script does not perform any actions with the caught exception, indicating its primary purpose is to test the behavior of these operations without causing a program crash. This code provides narrow functionality, focusing on the execution and error handling of specific JavaScript expressions.
